title: `-p` port mapping works on host's IP (v4 & v6) and 127.0.0.1, but not ::1
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** When a container is created inside a dual-stack podman network, ports exposed via `-p :` are reachable over the hosts's IP (both IPv4 and IPv6), but on localhost, they're only reachable on 127.0.0.1 and not ::1. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** (Everything here assumes rootfull podman usage) 1. (Assumption: podman host is dual-stack) 2. Create a dual-stack podman network: ```bash sudo podman network create --ipv6 --subnet fd00:1:2::/64 test ``` 3. Start a container in the new network: ```bash sudo podman run --rm -it -p 80:80 --network test nginx ``` 3. Attempt to access the forwarded port 80: ```bash curl -v4 http:// curl -v6 'http://[]' curl -v4 http://127.0.0.1 curl -v6 'http://[::1]' ``` **Describe the results you received:** Hitting port 80 on the host's IP (IPv4 or IPv6) and 127.0.0.1 all work, but connections to ::1 time out. **Describe the results you expected:** Hitting port 80 on ::1 should ideally work as well. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** The host is running Fedora 35 and podman with the default settings, except that firewalld has been removed (and no other firewalls are installed). The host has a fairly standard dual-stack setup: DHCP for IPv6 and SLAAC for IPv6. For IPv6, the host has a single address (no DHCP-PD subnets or anything like that).
Output of iptables-save (click to expand) ``` # Generated by iptables-save v1.8.7 on Thu Dec 23 02:56:32 2021 *filter :INPUT ACCEPT [1265:207409] :FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0] :OUTPUT ACCEPT [545:299708] :CNI-ADMIN - [0:0] :CNI-FORWARD - [0:0] -A INPUT -i cni-podman1 -p udp -m udp --dport 53 -j ACCEPT -A FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin rules" -j CNI-FORWARD -A CNI-FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin admin overrides" -j CNI-ADMIN -A CNI-FORWARD -d 10.89.0.2/32 -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT -A CNI-FORWARD -s 10.89.0.2/32 -j ACCEPT COMMIT # Completed on Thu Dec 23 02:56:32 2021 # Generated by iptables-save v1.8.7 on Thu Dec 23 02:56:32 2021 *nat :PREROUTING ACCEPT [493:155898] :INPUT ACCEPT [90:27116] :OUTPUT ACCEPT [16:1617] :POSTROUTING ACCEPT [17:1677] :CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 - [0:0] :CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 - [0:0] :CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT - [0:0] :CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ - [0:0] :CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK - [0:0] -A PREROUTING -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -A OUTPUT -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -A POSTROUTING -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd requiring masquerade" -j CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ -A POSTROUTING -s 10.89.0.2/32 -m comment --comment "name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -j CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 -A CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 -d 10.89.0.0/24 -m comment --comment "name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -j ACCEPT -A CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 ! -d 224.0.0.0/4 -m comment --comment "name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -j MASQUERADE -A CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -s 10.89.0.0/24 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -A CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -s 127.0.0.1/32 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -A CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j DNAT --to-destination 10.89.0.2:80 -A CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -p tcp -m comment --comment "dnat name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -m multiport --dports 80 -j CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -A CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ -m mark --mark 0x2000/0x2000 -j MASQUERADE -A CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd masquerade mark" -j MARK --set-xmark 0x2000/0x2000 COMMIT # Completed on Thu Dec 23 02:56:32 2021 ```
Output of ip6tables-save (click to expand) ``` # Generated by ip6tables-save v1.8.7 on Thu Dec 23 02:57:41 2021 *filter :INPUT ACCEPT [156:18543] :FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0] :OUTPUT ACCEPT [207:29587] :CNI-ADMIN - [0:0] :CNI-FORWARD - [0:0] -A FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin rules" -j CNI-FORWARD -A CNI-FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin admin overrides" -j CNI-ADMIN -A CNI-FORWARD -d fd00:1:2::2/128 -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT -A CNI-FORWARD -s fd00:1:2::2/128 -j ACCEPT COMMIT # Completed on Thu Dec 23 02:57:41 2021 # Generated by ip6tables-save v1.8.7 on Thu Dec 23 02:57:41 2021 *nat :PREROUTING ACCEPT [108:54257] :INPUT ACCEPT [0:0] :OUTPUT ACCEPT [30:3047] :POSTROUTING ACCEPT [32:3207] :CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 - [0:0] :CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 - [0:0] :CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT - [0:0] :CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ - [0:0] :CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK - [0:0] -A PREROUTING -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -A OUTPUT -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -A POSTROUTING -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd requiring masquerade" -j CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ -A POSTROUTING -s fd00:1:2::2/128 -m comment --comment "name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -j CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 -A CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 -d fd00:1:2::/64 -m comment --comment "name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -j ACCEPT -A CNI-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9a40 ! -d ff00::/8 -m comment --comment "name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -j MASQUERADE -A CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -s fd00:1:2::/64 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -A CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j DNAT --to-destination [fd00:1:2::2]:80 -A CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -p tcp -m comment --comment "dnat name: \"test\" id: \"123223b88e9b7b520b6a6004e831303b1e6d1be50023151363c1db42b2b1f0f5\"" -m multiport --dports 80 -j CNI-DN-74353d545f7bd7282ddd9 -A CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ -m mark --mark 0x2000/0x2000 -j MASQUERADE -A CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd masquerade mark" -j MARK --set-xmark 0x2000/0x2000 COMMIT # Completed on Thu Dec 23 02:57:41 2021 ```
**Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 3.4.4 API Version: 3.4.4 Go Version: go1.16.8 Built: Wed Dec 8 16:45:07 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:**
(Click to expand) ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.23.1 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - io - memory - hugetlb - pids - misc cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.0.30-2.fc35.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.30, commit: ' cpus: 8 distribution: distribution: fedora variant: server version: "35" eventLogger: journald hostname: idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 5.15.10-200.fc35.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 16173555712 memTotal: 16767184896 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.3-1.fc35.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.3 commit: 8e5757a4e68590326dafe8a8b1b4a584b10a1370 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.1.12-2.fc35.x86_64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.1.12 commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3 libslirp: 4.6.1 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.3 swapFree: 8485072896 swapTotal: 8485072896 uptime: 4m 21.68s plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: search: - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com - docker.io - quay.io store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 1 paused: 0 running: 1 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "true" imageStore: number: 1 runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.4.4 Built: 1638999907 BuiltTime: Wed Dec 8 16:45:07 2021 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.16.8 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.4.4 ```
**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** libvirt/KVM (no NAT; bridged to physical network; DHCP for IPv4, SLAAC for IPv6)

comments.body:
The linux kernel does not support this, also see https://www.cni.dev/plugins/current/meta/portmap/
Maybe you can try with `--network slirp4netns:enable_ipv6=true,port_handler=rootlesskit` as described here https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-create.1.md#--networkmode---net
Thanks for the info! (and the link to the portmap docs, I completely overlooked that)
